name: Product Affinity Analysis
description: Market basket analysis to discover which products are frequently purchased together. Identifies upsell and cross-sell opportunities, optimal product bundles, and natural product relationships. Uses lift metric to find statistically significant associations beyond random chance.
sql_template:
WITH product_pairs AS (
  SELECT 
    a.product_id AS product_a_id,
    a.product_title AS product_a,
    b.product_id AS product_b_id,
    b.product_title AS product_b,
    a.order_id
  FROM order_line_items a
  JOIN order_line_items b ON a.order_id = b.order_id AND a.product_id < b.product_id
  WHERE a.created_at >= '{{start_date}}'
    AND a.created_at < '{{end_date}}'
),
total_orders AS (
  SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS total
  FROM order_line_items
  WHERE created_at >= '{{start_date}}'
    AND created_at < '{{end_date}}'
),
product_frequencies AS (
  SELECT 
    product_id,
    product_title,
    COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_count
  FROM order_line_items
  WHERE created_at >= '{{start_date}}'
    AND created_at < '{{end_date}}'
  GROUP BY product_id, product_title
),
affinity_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    pp.product_a,
    pp.product_b,
    COUNT(DISTINCT pp.order_id) AS co_occurrence_count,
    pfa.order_count AS product_a_count,
    pfb.order_count AS product_b_count,
    to.total AS total_orders,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT pp.order_id) / to.total, 2) AS support_pct,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT pp.order_id) / pfa.order_count, 2) AS confidence_a_to_b,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT pp.order_id) / pfb.order_count, 2) AS confidence_b_to_a,
    ROUND(
      (COUNT(DISTINCT pp.order_id)::FLOAT / to.total) / 
      ((pfa.order_count::FLOAT / to.total) * (pfb.order_count::FLOAT / to.total)),
      2
    ) AS lift
  FROM product_pairs pp
  CROSS JOIN total_orders to
  JOIN product_frequencies pfa ON pfa.product_id = pp.product_a_id
  JOIN product_frequencies pfb ON pfb.product_id = pp.product_b_id
  GROUP BY pp.product_a, pp.product_b, pfa.order_count, pfb.order_count, to.total
)
SELECT 
  product_a,
  product_b,
  co_occurrence_count AS times_bought_together,
  support_pct,
  confidence_a_to_b AS pct_a_buyers_also_buy_b,
  confidence_b_to_a AS pct_b_buyers_also_buy_a,
  lift,
  CASE 
    WHEN lift > 3 THEN 'Very Strong'
    WHEN lift > 2 THEN 'Strong'
    WHEN lift > 1.5 THEN 'Moderate'
    WHEN lift > 1 THEN 'Weak'
    ELSE 'Negative'
  END AS affinity_strength
FROM affinity_metrics
WHERE lift >= {{min_lift}}
  AND co_occurrence_count >= {{min_co_occurrence}}
ORDER BY lift DESC, co_occurrence_count DESC
LIMIT {{limit}}

parameters.name: min_lift
parameters.label: Minimum Lift
parameters.description: Minimum lift score (1.5+ recommended for actionable insights)

parameters.name: min_co_occurrence
parameters.label: Minimum Co-Occurrences
parameters.description: Minimum times products must be bought together

parameters.name: limit
parameters.label: Number of Pairs
parameters.description: How many product pairs to show

author: Sery Team
example_output:
Returns a table with columns:
- `product_a`: First product name
- `product_b`: Second product name
- `times_bought_together`: Number of orders containing both
- `support_pct`: Percentage of all orders with this pair (0-100)
- `pct_a_buyers_also_buy_b`: Confidence A→B (% of product A buyers who also buy B)
- `pct_b_buyers_also_buy_a`: Confidence B→A (% of product B buyers who also buy A)
- `lift`: Lift score (>1 means positive association, >1.5 actionable)
- `affinity_strength`: Qualitative strength label

Interpretation:
- Lift = 1: Products purchased together at random chance rate
- Lift > 1: Products purchased together more than random
- Lift = 2: 2x more likely to be purchased together than random
- Lift > 3: Very strong association, excellent bundle/upsell candidate
